answer:
(i) Let's assume Marco can seat all his friends on $n-1$ tables. Since there are $n$ unique stickers, there must be at least one friend missing one sticker. Let's call this friend "Friend X." All other friends have completed their collections. Friend X can form a complete pair with any friend from the other tables. Therefore, Friend X needs to be seated at a new table, making it $n$ tables in total. \boxed{n}

(ii) With $n$ tables, each friend can have their own table, ensuring that no two friends have a complete collection together. This way, Marco can invite all his friends and achieve his desire. \boxed{n}
